author: ben_a_adams
content: i.e. if we don't define the criteria; how do we accept the result?

author: lightclient
content: account abstraction is the ability to abstract the validity of txs from the account and execute arbitrary code

author: lightclient
content: a lot of things fall out of that, such as what you listed

author: potuz
content: I think making an explicit list is a bit misleading as it frames the discussion in a set of very specific use cases. Yes, we could be "pragmatic" and change the tx types to enable these cases, and only these cases. On the other hand we could deliver a protocol that enables them all and those that we can't think about today.  If anything it'd be nice to have a list of tradeoffs that we're willing to make: are nodes going to be censoring some txs to not be dossed? Will centralized builders or trusted paymasters get all the POF and centralize the builder market even more?  Will it make FOCIL harder or will it make statelessness impossible? Etc
